body:
📍 For almost 5 years I worked in a digital agency, and I looked at clients' issues as a contractor. After that, I spent three more years working on the client side, and I noticed a huge difference in the approach. My findings and insides from the business are in the article below. Everything described here is purely my work experience in large corporations. And they could be considered as a list of recommendations for agencies. 1️⃣ Business mood of contractors is crucial for partnership between agencies & clients. Contractors from agencies are often psychologically unstable, and they got quickly deflated, especially when working with large advertisers. Only a few out of hundreds really do understand the essence of customer service (not taking things personally, not putting the client in stupid situations, knowing that "I do not know" is not the answer, making only realistic promises, etc.). Bottom line is that only few people know how to love a customer in a healthy way. The client will always be grateful for the reasonable autonomy and initiative by the contractor, for the simplification of processes in favor of speed and optimization. Because a good contractor is the merit of the client who works with him (it is the client who found good partners, it is the client who set up a process of interaction). Such agency helps to fulfill the plan, and allows the client to have full control. The contractor is the engine, not the cargo.
2️⃣ Agencies should understand well the clients' business goals. The big client always has the general sales plan (top tier) for the year/quarter/month, where digital ads is considered as one of many tools, and it has its share (below 100%). As a agency, you need to keep this in mind in your forecasts and plans, and you should have arguments why you won't reach or why you can reach a bigger share in the overall plan. The plan moves from top to bottom, and the adapted forecast moves from bottom to top.
3️⃣ Help the client to defend your ideas inside his company. When working on the client side, you always need to answer the questions like: "what will this advertising give us?", "will it be brand recognition or direct sales?", "how much will we spend?", "how much will we get?". So any digital plan will be defended equally with the offline media plan and equally with other plans. Each plan's utility ratio will be compared to each other in terms of cost/profit now/profit later. Therefore, it will not be redundant to choose the right arguments that will be clear to people who are not from digital.
4️⃣ Help corporations to go online. A lot of top managers know a lot about offline advertising and don't know how work with digital ads. There is a correlation with the age: those top managers who give money for advertising are usually 40+ years old. They usually do understand offline ads, but digital advertising is a black box for them. You have to be ready to explain complicated things with simple words, to translate arguments from the language of digital to the language of offline.
5️⃣ Find out how to count unique users. A lot of clients want to count unique users covered, and ideally they want to understand how much they've paid for the ad being shown to a real user (not a cookie). It's super important, but unrealizable in current realities. Not everyone understands that it's impossible to properly calculate (it's digital, damn!) those things, so we keep counting it approximately. Ideally, one should learn how to manage the distribution of ads between unique users, manage the price per ad being shown to the real person.
6️⃣ Adequate people on the client side are your friends. People who fully understand at least one small process and can manage it adequately are almost don't exist. In fast-growing companies the processes are often chaotic, and you get a mess of everything. A person who can make something turnkey (prepare, run, summarize the results, draw conclusions) is worth their weight in gold. Therefore, plan that processes on the client's side will not run smoothly.
7️⃣ Don't forget loyalty and retention users. It is important for businesses not only to attract customers, but also to retain them and to develop their check. For that purpose digital agencies often do not have enough energy or time, or it has low priority right now. But in the end, the greatest profit for the business comes from loyal clients, and that's why their total share should be constantly increased. The winner is the agency that looks ahead and offers the solution to retain users at the same as attract new ones. Don't forget to calculate it in your ad offers (but remember: it depends on clients' business).
Not all the businesses have these details, but those who work with digital advertising definitely have them. Use this information individually for your situation and pass it through a sieve of common sense.
title: 7 Insights from working in Digital Ads at customer side after working at an agency

body:
👀 Intro Hi there! I am currently working on a product closely related to cryptocurrency, blockchain and NFT, and am finding many points to explore. The cryptocurrency field is very fluid, with information updated daily. I am looking for answers and researching legislation, policies and ads systems. With this article, I am starting a series of posts about marketing in the cryptosphere where I will share information. I will update as I can, but no promises). 🗂 Specific My product is not tied to a specific country, so I am interested in running as extensive an advertisement as possible. But how do I organize the countries? I have grouped the countries into 3 groups. Not all countries are listed here, but only the main ones or especially interesting for selling cryptocurrencies for individuals and legal entities.
The first group includes the most cryptocurrency-loyal countries. Here there is usually a high competition in advertising auction => more expensive user cost and higher quality. In the second column are "undecided" countries, where there is no clear position on crypto or there are difficulties with public cryptocurrency payments. Here mining users is cheaper than in the first group, but their quality is also significant. The third block includes countries where cryptocurrency is banned or severely restricted. I recommend excluding these countries from advertising targeting, because there is a very low percentage of people who use cryptocurrency. At the same time, you can collect a large amount of traffic on the topic of crypto here. In each group, countries are filtered in descending order of GDP (nominal):
Priority 1 (Legalized) Priority 2 (Not prohibited) Priority 3 (Prohibition) USA UK China Japan Russia Indonesia Germany Brazil Turkey France Mexico Taiwan Italy Netherlands Iran Canada Saudi Arabia Vietnam South Korea Thailand Bangladesh Spain Sweden Egypt Australia Nigeria Pakistan Switzerland United Arab Emirates Algeria Belgium Ireland Morocco Austria Israel Bolivia Argentina Hong Kong Nepal Norway South Africa Afghanistan Philippines Denmark Singapore Colombia Colombia Portugal Chile Kazakhstan Finland Ecuador Czech Republic Dominican Republic New Zealand Uruguay Ukraine Lithuania Belorussia Jordan Estonia Cyprus El Salvador Iceland Georgia Armenia Malta 👉🏻 Conclusions Before you launch and pay for an ad in your chosen ad network, make sure it is positive about cryptocurrency advertising. Understanding the status of cryptocurrency in the country you want will help you build a competent strategy and save time on account unbundling. By segmenting your ad campaigns by authorized geo, you'll be able to get through moderation faster.
🕵🏻 Sources Official provisions regarding cryptocurrency and financial services:
Meta
Twitter
Google
Wishing you easy moderation and profitable traffic!
title: Countries for cryptoproducts marketing in 2022

body:
👨🏻‍💻 ICP (ideal customer profile) Examples of reference for searching audience:
📍 “Marketing analysts working in B2C companies with more than 100 employees located in the US. And they have a decent marketing budget, considering the fact that a company can afford to hire a dedicated marketing analyst. They currently manually export data from advertising and analytics tools or using proprietary tools that doesn’t work as expected. Then they spend hours on data cleansing and preparation. As a result, if they need a report they have to spend hours or days to get it.”
I single out 2 main groups of users who will be interested in the product. The column on the left are those who will set up and use it regularly. The column on the right is the decision maker and the people who will review the reports. The main pains of each group are described below.
Marketing analysts, marketers CMO, CTO, VP, CEO Collect reports by hand Need updated reports Dashboard exists but keeps crashing Need offline and online data in one place Integrations need to be configured manually Manual reports are tied to one person (risks) The sales cycle b2b product is long and you need to work with knowledge smoothly down the funnel. The strategy highlights the most effective channels for attracting. In the future, they can be supplemented by others (for example, Twitter, Video Display).
Here is a more detailed description of how to work with the marketing analysts segment.
📣 Marketing Funnel 1️⃣ Awareness
title: Marketing strategy for b2b SAAS in 2022

title: Example post

body:
Art of marketing blog A source code for hugo-based artofmarketing.blog
Creating / editing posts run in terminal make dev to run a blog on a localhost:1313 posts are in /content/posts folder (each post is a folder with a index.md file and optional pictures) - edit them and see realtime updates here localhost:1313 after editing old or creating new posts, go to terming, stop the local server (CTRL + C), and run make publish to sync updates to artofmarketing.blog Sync you local folder with github run in terminal make pull - this will sync your local folder with github
title: README
